name: copilot-budget
description: Track GitHub Copilot token usage and optionally append AI budget info to git commit messages
_engines_note: vscode ^1.103.0 is the earliest VS Code that bundles Node.js >= 22.16, the version that promoted node:sqlite from experimental to stable (Electron 37, Node 22.17.0). Required by src/otelReader.ts.

copilot-budget.enabled.description: Enable or disable Copilot Budget token usage tracking

copilot-budget.sessionMaxAgeDays.markdownDescription: Only scan Copilot chat session files modified within this many days. Older sessions are skipped — their tokens are already folded into the session baseline, so re-reading them gains nothing and is slow when the chat history is large. Set to `0` to disable the filter (scan everything).

copilot-budget.commitHook.enabled.description: Automatically install the prepare-commit-msg hook to append AI budget info to commits

copilot-budget.commitHook.trailers.estim.markdownDescription: Opt-in git trailer key for estimated USD cost (e.g. `"Copilot-Est-Cost"`). Disabled by default; enable for users who want USD in commit history. AI Credits is the primary unit.

copilot-budget.commitHook.trailers.aiCre.markdownDescription: Git trailer key for total AI Credits (1 AIC = $0.01, plan-invariant). Set to `false` to disable.
Git trailer key for per-model AI Credits breakdown. Set to a string (e.g. `"Copilot-AI-Credits-Models"`) to enable.

copilot-budget.displayCurrency.markdownDescription: Unit used for status bar, tooltip, and panel. `aic` shows AI Credits (1 AIC = $0.01, plan-invariant). `usd` converts to dollars at the same rate. Commit trailer values are always bare numbers (AIC for `Copilot-AI-Credits`, USD for the opt-in `Copilot-Est-Cost`) regardless of this setting. Stored at user (application) scope so the choice persists across windows.
